title: cordylanthus maritimus subsp. palustris
intro: Cordylanthus maritimus subsp. palustris, also known as the Alkali Cord Root or Pacific Blue-eyed Mary, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family, Plantaginaceae. It is native to the coastal areas of the Pacific Northwest region in North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and is used to treat a range of health issues such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including sore throats, colds, and fever.
